title: UCB — Relationships
html:
UCB — ZoomRx Relationship

Governing Thought

UCB is a mid-tenure, expanding ZoomRx account — approximately 4 years deep with a service footprint that has grown from single-brand studies (Briviact) to multi-TA tracking across immunology, epilepsy, and neuromuscular disease. The relationship is not yet at preferred-vendor depth (no MSA, no franchise-level ownership) but is demonstrating the trajectory pattern that precedes it: each new brand engagement (Bimzelx Derm PET → Bimzelx Rheum PET → Fintepla PET → gMG HCP-Pt) widens the embed across both TAs and project types.

Partnership Timeline

Year Milestone
~2022 Relationship established; Briviact Non-User Study launched — first ZoomRx engagement with UCB
2022-2023 Briviact Hospital Integrated Study; Briviact & Nayzilam PET Studies begin — epilepsy becomes first tracking TA
2024 Bimzelx HCP-Patient Conversations (immunology) — first engagement outside epilepsy; gMG HCP-Patient Conversations (Rystiggo/Zilbrysq) — third TA opens
2025 Fintepla PET launches (with Qual component); Nayzilam & Staccato ATU (Wave 1) — epilepsy engagement deepens to 3 project types
2026 (Jan-May) Bimzelx Dermatology PET kicks off (W1 Q1-Q2 2026); Bimzelx Rheumatology PET initiated — immunology PET established; TA Knowledge uploaded to SharePoint (3 TAs)

Key trajectory: Each year has added either a new TA or a new project type. The progression from single-study (2022) to multi-TA tracking (2026) follows the pattern seen in accounts that become franchise-level engagements.

Why UCB Works with ZoomRx

Pillar 1: Therapeutic Diversity Requires a Versatile Partner

UCB's two-franchise model (Neurology + Immunology) means they need a research partner comfortable across fundamentally different HCP audiences — dermatologists, rheumatologists, epileptologists, neuromuscular specialists, and pediatric neurologists. ZoomRx's cross-TA capability allows UCB to use one vendor across these diverse audiences rather than managing multiple specialty research firms.

Pillar 2: PET as the Anchor

PET (Promotional Effectiveness Tracking) has been the entry point for both Bimzelx and the epilepsy portfolio. ZoomRx's proprietary PET methodology and benchmark data create continuity across waves and brands. For UCB, this means comparable metrics across Bimzelx, Fintepla, and Briviact — enabling portfolio-level insight even though the brands serve entirely different patient populations.

Pillar 3: HCP-Patient Conversation Depth

The Bimzelx and gMG HCP-Patient Conversations studies represent a capability that goes beyond tracking — they capture real-world treatment decision dynamics. For UCB, this is particularly valuable in gMG (complex mechanism choice between FcRn and C5) and immunology (dual skin+joint positioning decisions). This qualitative depth differentiates ZoomRx from tracking-only vendors.

Proven Impact: Case Studies

1. Bimzelx HCP-Patient Conversations (Immunology)

Challenge: UCB needed to understand how dermatologists discuss Bimzelx with psoriasis patients in real practice — particularly around the scalp psoriasis and joint symptom screening moments that drive prescribing decisions. Approach: ZoomRx captured and analyzed real HCP-patient conversations, coding for discussion themes, objections, treatment triggers, and patient concerns. Impact: Revealed that scalp involvement was discussed in 57% of conversations and was the most common special site driving treatment decisions. Found that only 1 of 7 PsO+PsA patients was co-managed with rheumatology — meaning dermatologists are the primary PsA decision-makers, a critical insight for Bimzelx's dual skin+joint positioning.

2. Fintepla PET with Qual (Epilepsy)

Challenge: UCB needed both quantitative tracking of Fintepla rep effectiveness against Epidiolex and qualitative understanding of prescribing barriers in the rare epilepsy space. Approach: ZoomRx ran an integrated PET + Qual study, combining wave-based SOV and message tracking with in-depth qualitative research on physician decision-making in Dravet/LGS. Impact: Provided dual-lens insight — quantitative competitive positioning data alongside qualitative understanding of REMS monitoring burden perceptions and referral pathway dynamics.

3. Nayzilam & Staccato ATU (Epilepsy)

Challenge: UCB needed awareness, trial, and usage tracking for rescue therapies (Nayzilam and Staccato) in the seizure cluster market — a niche segment with different HCP and caregiver dynamics. Approach: ZoomRx fielded an ATU study covering awareness, prescribing intent, and patient/caregiver experience across rescue medication options. Impact: Established baseline metrics for the rescue therapy market and identified awareness gaps that informed field force messaging priorities.

Growth Trajectory

The relationship is expanding along three vectors:

1. Immunology PET Franchise Build-Out

Bimzelx Dermatology PET (active) and Bimzelx Rheumatology PET (kick-off) represent the transition from ad hoc studies to ongoing tracking. As Bimzelx expands into HS and potentially additional indications, each new HCP audience creates a natural PET extension.

2. Epilepsy Portfolio Deepening

Fintepla's pipeline (Rett syndrome submission, 4th epilepsy indication) will generate new tracking needs for audiences ZoomRx hasn't yet served at UCB (pediatric neurologists for Rett). The Neurona cell therapy acquisition signals long-term epilepsy investment.

3. Service Type Expansion

The current footprint is PET + HCP-Pt + ATU. The entire MSL/MEE, FRM, KAM, Message Testing, Digital Tracking, and Ferma Congress suite is untapped. Any single one of these becoming active would represent a meaningful revenue step-up.

Competitive Threats to the Relationship

Threat 1: UCB's In-House Evidence Capabilities

UCB's Patient Evidence function (now led by Emmanuel Caeymaex, former CCO) may be building internal analytics and tracking capabilities. The CCO→Patient Evidence leadership swap signals the company is investing in this function. If UCB internalizes basic tracking, ZoomRx must differentiate on methodology and benchmark data.

Mitigation: Position ZoomRx's 700M+ benchmark data points and cross-client normative context as irreplaceable — internal teams cannot replicate industry benchmarks.

Threat 2: Single-Project Relationships in gMG

The gMG engagement is a single completed HCP-Pt study. Without follow-on work, this TA risks going dormant. Rystiggo and Zilbrysq are both growth brands (+65% YoY for Rystiggo) that should be generating ongoing tracking needs.

Mitigation: Proactively propose gMG PET or MEE work. The Vyvgart competitive dynamic and UCB's dual-mechanism positioning are natural tracking narratives.

Key Takeaway for New Principals

UCB is an account in the "prove and expand" phase — established enough to have multi-TA engagement but not yet deep enough to be entrenched. The priority is converting the current PET anchor into a franchise-level tracking relationship across both Immunology and Neurology, while proactively filling the service gaps (MSL/MEE, FRM, KAM) before a competitor establishes in those spaces.

Back to Accounts Hub | Home
